_doc: D-237 estimate-provenance guard config. See docs/DESIGN-D237-lint-guard.md. Curated + grow: add files as the silent-fallback sweep expands the audited surface.
_declaredDefaultProviders_doc: Functions whose SOLE job is to supply a default. Their internal literal fallback is the sanctioned source; the obligation to DECLARE falls on callers that persist/render the value. Keep this list SHORT and auditable.
_provenanceTokens_doc: If a fallback's NEAREST enclosing function references any of these, the estimate is DECLARED and passes. Must be a SPECIFIC estimate-provenance vocabulary — NOT generic `_method`/`confidence`, which collide with unrelated fields (match_method, match_confidence) and mask real silent fallbacks. Extend deliberately as new declaration markers land.
_defaultNamePattern_doc: A fallback VALUE that is an identifier/property whose name matches this is a 'named default' (REF_SPW, defaults.mpw, DISTANCE_DEFAULTS.marathon) and is in scope just like a numeric literal.
_ignoreValues_doc: Numeric fallbacks equal to these are treated as 'empty/none' sentinels, not fabricated estimates (e.g. `x ?? 0`, `Number(v) || 0`). 0 only by default.
_knownExceptions_doc: EXPLICITLY ACKNOWLEDGED open bugs — NOT declared-OK. Matched by file+fallback text (line-independent). The guard lists them loudly as KNOWN-UNRESOLVED with their ticket and does NOT count them as failures, so CI can be made required and only NEW silent fallbacks block. Each MUST carry a ticket. Remove the entry when the ticket lands. This is the opposite of the band-aid: it flags the bug as open, it does not hide it behind a disclosure.

knownExceptions.reason: C3 — readiness must gate on a SET race goal, not default to marathon; discipline-aware. Fix is gating, not declaration.
C3 — marathon-default long-run target for a no-goal / non-running athlete.
C3 — marathon-default weekly-mileage target for a no-goal / non-running athlete.
